content:
# Matthew Chapter 17 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_017_page_001.jpg, MAT_017_page_002.jpg

1. And after six days the Yeshua takes along the Petros and Iakobos and Ioannes the brother of him, and leads them up into a high mountain by themselves.

2. And he was transfigured before them, and the face of him shone as the sun, and the garments of him became white as the light.

3. And behold, there appeared to them Moses and Elias, conversing with him.

4. And answering, the Petros said to the Yeshua, "Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you wish, I will make here three tents, one for you and one for Moses and one for Elias."

5. While he was still speaking, behold, a cloud of light overshadowed them, and behold, a voice out of the cloud, saying, "This is the Son of me, the beloved, in whom I am well-pleased; hear him."

6. And having heard, the disciples fell upon the face of them and were exceedingly afraid.

7. And the Yeshua came and having touched them said, "Rise and do not fear."

8. And having lifted the eyes of them, they saw no one except the Yeshua himself alone.

9. And of them descending from the mountain, the Yeshua commanded them, saying, "Tell no one the vision until the Son of the Man is raised from [the] dead."

10. And the disciples asked him, saying, "Why then do the scribes say that Elias must come first?"

11. And answering, he said, "Elias indeed comes and will restore all things.

12. But I say to you that Elias already came, and they did not recognize him but did to him whatever they wished. So also the Son of the Man is about to suffer by them."

13. Then the disciples understood that concerning Ioannes the Baptizer he spoke to them.

14. And of them having come to the crowd, a man came to him, kneeling to him

15. and saying, "Lord, have mercy on the son of me, for he is epileptic and suffers badly; for often he falls into the fire and often into the water.

16. And I brought him to the disciples of you, and they were not able to heal him."

17. And answering, the Yeshua said, "O faithless and perverted generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I bear with you? Bring him here to me."

18. And the Yeshua rebuked him, and the demon came out from him, and the boy was healed from that hour.

19. Then having come, the disciples to the Yeshua privately said, "Why were we not able to cast it out?"

20. And he says to them, "Because of the little-faith of you. For truly I say to you, if you have faith as a grain of mustard, you will say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move, and nothing will be impossible for you."

21. [This verse is absent in Sinaiticus.]

22. And of them gathering together in the Galilaia, the Yeshua said to them, "The Son of the Man is about to be delivered into [the] hands of men,

23. and they will kill him, and on the third day he will be raised." And they were exceedingly grieved.

24. And of them having come to Capernaum, the [ones] receiving the didrachma came to the Petros and said, "The teacher of you, does he not pay the didrachma?"

25. He says, "Yes." And having come into the house, the Yeshua anticipated him, saying, "What do you think, Simon? The kings of the earth, from whom do they receive customs or poll-tax? From the sons of them or from the strangers?"

26. And having said, "From the strangers," the Yeshua said to him, "Then the sons are free.

27. But in order that we may not scandalize them, having gone to [the] sea, cast a hook, and the fish coming up first, take; and having opened the mouth of it, you will find a stater. Having taken that, give to them for me and you."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **μετεμορφώθη (v.2):** "Was transfigured" — from μεταμορφόω, a transformation of form. The same word Paul uses in Romans 12:2 and 2 Corinthians 3:18. The divine glory shines through Jesus' human form.

2. **v.5 — The divine voice:** "This is my beloved Son... hear him" — echoes the baptism declaration (3:17) with the addition of "hear him" (ἀκούετε αὐτοῦ), alluding to Deuteronomy 18:15, the promise of a prophet like Moses whom Israel must hear.

3. **v.21 — Absent in Sinaiticus:** Many later manuscripts add "But this kind does not go out except by prayer and fasting." Sinaiticus omits this verse entirely. It appears to be a later scribal addition influenced by Mark 9:29.

4. **δίδραχμα (v.24):** "Didrachma" — the two-drachma temple tax, equivalent to half a shekel. Required annually of every Jewish male for the upkeep of the temple (Exodus 30:13).

5. **στατήρ (v.27):** "Stater" — a coin worth four drachmas, exactly enough to pay the temple tax for two persons (Jesus and Peter).

— EMET, AI Translator
